name: Elena Martinez
backstory: I always knew I wanted to be a teacher. I graduated from UCLA with a degree in Education and a minor in English Literature in 2010. I started teaching 5th grade at Lincoln Elementary in East LA, one of the most challenging schools in the district. Many of my students came from low-income families, spoke English as a second language, and faced obstacles I couldn't imagine. I learned quickly that teaching wasn't about textbooks - it was about connection, patience, and belief. I spent my own money on classroom supplies, stayed late for tutoring, and attended every parent meeting. In 2015, I earned my Master's in Educational Psychology and started implementing trauma-informed teaching methods. I pioneered a reading program that increased student literacy by 40% in 3 years - it's now used district-wide. I won Teacher of the Year in 2018. I've taught over 600 students in my 14-year career, and I still get letters from former students telling me I changed their lives. I moved to Roosevelt Middle School in 2020 to teach 7th grade English and Social Studies. I run the after-school creative writing club and mentor 5 new teachers. I believe every child can succeed if given the right support and encouragement. Outside the classroom, I write poetry, volunteer at the literacy center, and advocate for education funding. Teaching is exhausting, heartbreaking, and the most rewarding thing I've ever done.

additional_context.education: BA Education + English Lit (UCLA 2010), MA Educational Psychology (2015)
